author: ankitvgupta
text: torch.multinomial without replacement returns repetitive values when all non-zero items are exhausted

author: ankitvgupta
text:
## 🐛 Bug

When given a one-hot probability distribution, with replacement=False, torch.multinomial returns 0s for all indices after the first one. While I understand that it is weird to sample > 1 value without replacement if the distribution is one-hot, I think this is less desirable behavior than randomly sampling the remaining values without replacement.

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

```
>>> torch.multinomial(torch.FloatTensor([0,1,0,0]), 3, replacement=False)
tensor([1, 0, 0])
```

This seems undesired, because replacement=False implies to me that I should expect my outputs to all be different. My understanding is that this was discussed in a recent issue about the GPU kernel for multinomial not matching the CPU here: [#9062](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/9062)

## Expected behavior

I expected not to get the index "0" in the output more than once.
